mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.9% in April 2010 indicates post-crisis labor market recovery phase
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero signals extreme monetary accommodation. When FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, equity risk premium compresses and investors shift capital from fixed income to equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.2% in April 2010 reflects post-crisis zero lower bound policy
regime_hint: monetary emergency accommodation at zero lower bound
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand and economic activity. When RSAFS increases month-over-month by more than 0.5%, forward earnings expectations rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 366.4 billion in April 2010 reflects post-recession consumer spending recovery
failure_condition: When RSAFS grows > 0.5% MoM, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. When INDPRO accelerates above its 3-month trend, it signals robust supply-side expansion and profit margin improvement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.27 in April 2010 shows post-crisis industrial recovery
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with expanding capacity utilization
failure_condition: When INDPRO accelerates above 3-month trend, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening. When both PAYEMS increases and UNRATE falls simultaneously, corporate hiring confidence and wage growth expectations rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.16M and UNRATE at 9.9% in April 2010 reflect early labor market recovery
regime_hint: synchronized labor market tightening in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS grows > 0.1% MoM and UNRATE falls > 0.1pp, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals broad-based demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors. When both accelerate, forward GDP growth expectations rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 626K and RSAFS at 366.4B in March-April 2010 reflect post-crisis demand recovery
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: When both HOUST grows > 2% MoM and RSAFS grows > 0.3% MoM, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% annually signals monetary policy normalization risk. In regimes where FEDFUNDS is near zero, rising inflation expectations trigger equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.73 in March 2010 with FEDFUNDS at 0.2% creates policy normalization tension
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and profit margin compression. When INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average in a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%), it predicts negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 6-month moving average AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.27 and CPIAUCSL at 217.73 in April 2010 show early recovery with contained inflation
failure_condition: When INDPRO falls below 6-month MA and CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%, SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05).
